decisions.title: Live computation over persisted rollup
decisions.decision: Rollup is computed on demand by reading SHAPE.json, feature directories, and Work Order files. No persisted initiative-rollup artifact.
decisions.rationale: Initiatives have 3-10 candidate features. Reading a handful of JSON files is cheap. Persisting would introduce another sync mechanism and staleness risk.

decisions.title: Cancelled status preserved as-is
decisions.decision: Work Order status 'cancelled' is surfaced directly in the initiative view, not mapped to 'parked'.
decisions.rationale: 'parked' means intentionally deferred before or outside execution; 'cancelled' means work started and was abandoned. The distinction is semantically useful.

decisions.title: New module in orchestration package
decisions.decision: Code lives in .cnogo/scripts/workflow/orchestration/initiative_rollup.py. CLI subcommands (initiative-show, initiative-list) added to workflow_memory.py.
decisions.rationale: Initiative rollup is above Work Orders conceptually — it reads shapes and aggregates work orders. It should not be inside work_order.py or workflow_memory.py.

decisions.title: Automatic initiative section in /status and /resume
decisions.decision: When the current feature has a parentShape link (in CONTEXT.json or FEATURE.json), /status and /resume include a compact initiative section by default. No flag required.
decisions.rationale: If a parentShape link exists, the user opted into shape workflow. The section is cheap to compute and always relevant.

decisions.title: Review outcome as compact reviewVerdict field
decisions.decision: Per-feature review signal in the rollup uses a compact reviewVerdict field with values: pending|pass|warn|fail. Full review findings are not inlined.
decisions.rationale: The initiative view should show signal, not noise. Users can drill into individual features for full review details.

decisions.title: Unified status mapping across shape and execution phases
decisions.decision: Status is derived from the furthest-progressed artifact: no FEATURE stub = SHAPE candidate status (draft/parked/blocked); FEATURE stub = discuss-ready; CONTEXT exists = discussing; PLAN exists without WO = planned; WO exists = WO status (implementing/reviewing/shipping/blocked/completed/cancelled).
decisions.rationale: Provides a single coherent view across the full lifecycle from shaping through execution.

decisions.title: Read-only on SHAPE.json
decisions.decision: The rollup never writes to SHAPE.json. shapeFeedback is collected from child CONTEXT.json files and displayed as pending items. Only /shape writes to SHAPE.json.
decisions.rationale: Separation of concerns. The rollup is a read-only aggregation view; /shape is the write path for initiative-level decisions.

constraints: Python stdlib only — no external dependencies
Read-only on SHAPE.json — rollup never mutates shape artifacts
Work Orders are the source of truth for execution status — rollup reads, never duplicates
Backward compatible — features without parentShape are unaffected
Validation coverage — workflow_validate.py should verify rollup consistency

openQuestions.question: Should initiative-list also scan for orphaned features (features with parentShape pointing to a deleted/moved SHAPE.json)?
Should the initiative-level next action consider recommendedSequence ordering, or just report on whatever is furthest behind?

relatedCode.description: Work Order model — source of execution status (planned/implementing/reviewing/shipping/blocked/completed/cancelled), build_work_order() function
SHAPE.json validation — candidateFeatures schema, discuss-ready stub requirements, shape feedback validation
FEATURE.json stub validation — parentShape linkage, staleness checks, contract link validation
CLI entrypoint — will host initiative-show and initiative-list subcommands
Rendering engine — renders FEATURE.json and CONTEXT.json to Markdown, including parentShape sections
Status command — needs initiative section when parentShape link is detected
Resume command — needs initiative section when parentShape link is detected
Shape command — creates SHAPE.json with candidateFeatures and FEATURE.json stubs
